title: Participle clauses
explanation:
Participle clauses

Participle clauses make sentences shorter and more formal.

  • Feeling tired, she went to bed early.
  • Built in 1890, the theatre is still in use.
  • Having finished the report, he left the office.

questions.text: [*Having finished|Finished|To finish] the meeting, we went for coffee.
questions.feedback: Perfect participle shows the first action happened before the second.

questions.text: [*Built|Building|Having built] in the 18th century, the bridge is still standing.
questions.feedback: Past participle clause describes the bridge.

questions.text: [*Feeling|Felt|To feel] unwell, she left work early.
questions.feedback: Present participle clause describes the subject's state.

questions.text: [*Not knowing|Not known|Did not know] what to say, he stayed silent.
[*Having been told|Telling|Being tell] the news, they changed their plans.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: ____ for over an hour, we finally gave up. | *Having waited | Waited | To wait
questions.feedback: The waiting happened before giving up.

questions.text: ____ by his colleagues, he felt more confident. | *Encouraged | Encouraging | Having encourage
____ the instructions carefully, she avoided mistakes. | *Having read | Read | To read
____ any better idea, we followed his plan. | *Not having | No having | Not have
[*Walking home|Walked home|To walk home], I saw an old friend.
questions.feedback: Present participle clause for simultaneous action.

questions.text: The documents, [*signed by the director|signing by the director|having sign by the director], were sent immediately.
[*Having lost|Losting|To lose] his keys, he could not get in.
[*Born|Being born|Having born] in Canada, she speaks both English and French.
questions.feedback: Past participle clause for background fact.

questions.text: [*Realising|Realised|To realise] the time, we rushed to the station.
